title: Implementation discipline
summary: Implementation MUST remain within the approved plan, establish deterministic pre-edit repository-check baselines, and directly remediate every cause of a retry.
instructions: Each material change MUST map to an acceptance criterion or an explicitly documented enabling change.
Agents MUST NOT redesign adjacent systems unless the approved plan requires it.

Before the first coder invocation, the harness MUST run and persist each configured repository-check profile referenced by deterministic stage gates so pre-existing failures are distinguishable from regressions.

The coder MUST inspect the pre-implementation static and fast baselines before editing. Existing failures MUST be repaired when bounded and low-risk; an unchanged pre-existing failure MUST NOT block the workflow when remediation would be broad, structural, or unrelated to the approved change. Any new or worsened diagnostic MUST remain a hard failure.

On implementation attempt 2 or later, the coder MUST perform work that directly addresses every issue causing the retry and MUST populate implementation.remediation with cause, action, and evidence. A paperwork-only retry is prohibited.
Agents SHOULD run the narrowest useful checks while iterating, and the harness MUST rerun declared deterministic gates independently.

Before submission, the implementation record, changed-file claims, retry-remediation evidence, acceptance evidence, and referenced summary artifact MUST run their resolved policy-bound validators under VALID-001.
